title: Ensure that the --insecure-experimental-approve-all-kubelet-csrs- for-group argument is not set

input_row.title: Ensure that the --insecure-experimental-approve-all-kubelet-csrs- for-group argument is not set
input_row.rationale: Setting the --insecure-experimental-approve-all-kubelet-csrs-for-group flag circumvents the desired “approval” process. All the certificates are auto-approved without checking their integrity. This flag is meant to be used for development and testing purposes only and hence should not be used in the production.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the master node: ps -ef | grep kube-controller-manager Verify that the --insecure-experimental-approve-all-kubelet-csrs-for-group argument is not set.
input_row.remediation: Edit the /etc/kubernetes/controller-manager file on the master node and remove the -- insecure-experimental-approve-all-kubelet-csrs-for-group argument from the KUBE_CONTROLLER_MANAGER_ARGS parameter. Based on your system, restart the kube-controller-manager service. For example: systemctl restart kube-controller-manager.service Impact: Invalid certificates will be rejected. Default Value: By default, --insecure-experimental-approve-all-kubelet-csrs-for-group is not set.   81 | P a g e References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-controller-manager/ 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kubelet-tls-bootstrapping/#auto-approval
input_row.impact: Invalid certificates will be rejected. Default Value: By default, --insecure-experimental-approve-all-kubelet-csrs-for-group is not set.   81 | P a g e

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
This control requires verification that the kube-controller-manager process is not started with the --insecure-experimental-approve-all-kubelet-csrs-for-group argument. This flag is set as a process argument or in a static pod manifest or systemd unit file on the host running the kube-controller-manager. Kubernetes does not expose controller manager process arguments or flags via the Kubernetes API or kubectl. There is no Kubernetes API object or field that reveals this flag's presence or absence. The audit procedure explicitly instructs to check the running process or configuration file on the host, which requires OS-level access.

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. SSH to the master node(s) running the kube-controller-manager.
2. Run the following command to list the kube-controller-manager process and its arguments:
   ```
   ps -ef | grep kube-controller-manager
   ```
3. Examine the process arguments and verify that --insecure-experimental-approve-all-kubelet-csrs-for-group is NOT present.
4. If the kube-controller-manager is deployed as a static pod, check the manifest file (commonly at /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-controller-manager.yaml) and ensure the argument is not present in the command or args section.
5. If the flag is not set in any process or configuration, the control is compliant. If present, remediate by removing the flag and restarting the kube-controller-manager.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES 1.6 BENCHMARK V1.0.0 ARCHIVE
